genre_refs: App Store / Google Play app-promo ad
description: Horizontal app-promotion graphic for the CBP Home mobile app, in Spanish, in navy blue, gold/yellow, white, and red. At left is a smartphone mockup showing the app's welcome screen: a US flag and 'Official App of the U.S. Department of Homeland Security' header, the DHS seal, an illustrated officer icon, the heading 'Welcome To CBP Home', subtext 'A single portal to multiple CBP services to streamline your experience', and a red 'LOGIN OR SIGN UP' button with 'with LOGIN.GOV'. The background is a tinted blue photo of hands holding a phone. Large white bold display text reads 'CBP Home App' with a gold subtitle bar 'El portal unico que agiliza muchos servicios de la CBP'. A body paragraph in Spanish describes the 'Intencion de Salir' (Intent to Depart) feature, stating it offers aliens who are illegally in the country, or whose parole has been revoked, a voluntary, orderly, defined process to notify the government of their intention to leave the United States. The 'U.S. Customs and Border Protection' seal/wordmark sits at lower right, with App Store and Google Play download badges at top right. The tweet states that those who self-deport via the app receive $1,000 upon confirmed return home.
notable_text: CBP Home App; El portal unico que agiliza muchos servicios de la CBP; Intencion de Salir; Welcome To CBP Home; LOGIN OR SIGN UP
